The soul of Bohol, simmered slow.

Abl Bulalohan is a beloved local table where beef marrow soup is coaxed into golden comfort — served with warm smiles, cold beer, and the unhurried rhythm of the Bohol coast.

Our Pride

The Bulalo, Done Right

Born in the cattle country of Southern Luzon, bulalo is beef shank and bone marrow slow-simmered until the broth turns clear, golden and velvety. We honor that tradition pot by pot.

Beef shank with marrow, sweet corn on the cob, pechay, potato and a whisper of ginger meet fish sauce and patience — then arrive at your table with rice and a calamansi-soy dip on the side.

  • ⏳Three-hour simmer for a broth that melts instead of sits.
  • 🦴Bone-in beef shank with the buttery marrow you scoop.
  • 🌽Sweet corn & pechay folded in at the very end.
  • 🍋Calamansi & patis on the side — the Filipino finish.

Our Story

A roadside kitchen, warmed by regulars

Set along the Bohol Circumferential Road in Jagna, Abl Bulalohan began as a small family spot where early diners sometimes found themselves the only ones in the room — and left as friends. The bulalo did the talking.

Today the pot still simmers the same way: slow, generous and unpretentious. Visitors exploring Canuba Beach, Kinahugan Falls and the old St. Michael Archangel Church stop by for a bowl, a cold beer and the kind of welcome that keeps them coming back.

Warmth

A table that feels like home.

Fresh

Market-day produce, daily.

Value

Honest prices, full bowls.

A Glimpse

"We found this super cute spot just a short walk from where we're staying. We were a little nervous at first — we were the only ones there! But we eat early, and the bulalo was wonderful. A true hidden gem."

John & Heather Bacon
